subject: [PATCH v7 06/15] mm: add MM_CP_UFFD_RWP change_protection() flag

2026-06-29.patch_summary: This patch adds two new flags to the change_protection() function in the Linux kernel: MM_CP_UFFD_RWP and MM_CP_UFFD_RWP_RESOLVE. These flags are used for userfaultfd (UFFD) read-write protection (RWP). The MM_CP_UFFD_RWP flag installs a PAGE_NONE PTE with the uffd bit set, while the MM_CP_UFFD_RWP_RESOLVE flag restores the original vma->vm_page_prot and clears the uffd bit. These flags are used consistently across various memory types, including anonymous, shared memory, transparent huge pages (THP), and hugetlb. The patch also updates several kernel files to incorporate these new flags.
